description: The Contini clan is a powerful Neapolitan Camorra clan operating in Naples, Italy, founded in the early 1980s by Edoardo Contini. It is a founding member of the Secondigliano Alliance and is active in drug trafficking, extortion, and money laundering. The clan has a strong presence in Europe, including Spain, the Netherlands, and Eastern Europe. It is considered one of the most powerful Camorra groups due to its lack of internal splits and informants.

country_links.context: The Contini clan obtained cocaine from narco-traffickers in Colombia.

country_links.context: The Contini clan had a money launderer operating in Santo Domingo, Dominican Republic.

country_links.evidence_quote: The clan is also active outside Italy, particularly in Barcelona , Spain, in Amsterdam , Netherlands, in Edinburgh , Scotland , and in Eastern Europe .
country_links.context: The Contini clan is active in Amsterdam, Netherlands.
